natural_language: Five blocks are on the table in this arrangement. To your left, there's a short stack: B sitting on top of C, and that C is directly on the table. Over on the right, another pair: D rests on E, with E touching the table. Right in the middle, block A is all alone on the table. The exposed surfaces belong to B (atop the left column), D (atop the right column), and lone A. The mechanical hand is idle. I want you to make one change: move A so it sits on B. Everything else must stay exactly as it is — D stays on E, C stays under B, nobody moves except A. Don't over-complicate it.
